pattern: Add edges one by one into a union-find structure; the first edge whose endpoints already share a root is the cycle-closing edge to remove.
hints: The extra edge is exactly the one that joins two vertices that were already connected by earlier edges.
A disjoint-set (union-find) structure can tell you in near-constant time whether two vertices are already in the same component.
Process the edges in input order; return the first edge whose two endpoints already share a root, which is automatically the last such edge overall since only one cycle exists.

edge_inputs.description: triangle, last edge closes the cycle
cycle among first four nodes, fifth dangles
redundant edge appears early in the list
longer path with closing edge last
self-referential star where extra connects two leaves

stress.description: random spanning tree on n nodes plus one extra non-tree edge appended last
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(3, max(3, size))
    edges = []
    nodes = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nodes)
    existing = set()
    for i in range(1, n):
        a = nodes[i]
        b = nodes[rng.randint(0, i - 1)]
        u, v = min(a, b), max(a, b)
        edges.append([u, v])
        existing.add((u, v))
    while True:
        a = rng.randint(1, n)
        b = rng.randint(1, n)
        if a == b:
            continue
        u, v = min(a, b), max(a, b)
        if (u, v) not in existing:
            edges.append([u, v])
            break
    return [edges]
stress.note: exactly one cycle, extra edge added at the end
